framing: Laid-off e-commerce merchandiser at Farfetch seeking digital product role at luxury fashion house in Paris

jd.title: Digital Merchandising Manager
jd.style_note: Vague/culture-first: luxury brand tone, emphasis on taste and instinct, light on technical requirements

requirements.text: You understand luxury — not just the products, but the codes, the storytelling, and the emotional connection with clients
5+ years of experience in digital merchandising, e-commerce buying, or online retail strategy
You know how to read data and use it to curate — not just optimise, but create desirability
You've managed assortment planning for fashion categories across multiple markets
Bonus: experience with Salesforce Commerce Cloud, SAP Hybris, or similar e-commerce platforms
Bonus: experience in luxury (LVMH, Kering, Richemont, or independent luxury brands)

responsibilities: Own digital merchandising strategy for Gucci.com across EMEA markets
Curate online product assortments by category, season, and market — balancing commercial KPIs with brand storytelling
Collaborate with buying, creative, and marketing teams on product launches and editorial content placement
Analyse sell-through rates, conversion funnels, and inventory levels to optimise product visibility and pricing
Lead A/B testing and personalisation initiatives for product discovery and recommendation engines

expected_output.summary: E-commerce merchandising professional with 6 years of experience at Farfetch (London), most recently Senior Merchandiser managing womenswear category for UK and EU markets. Made redundant in January 2026 during Farfetch's post-acquisition restructuring under Coupang. Managed GBP 45M annual GMV across 200+ luxury brand partners. Expert in marketplace merchandising, sell-through analytics, and product curation. Deep knowledge of luxury fashion market. Seeking transition from marketplace to direct-to-consumer luxury brand.

experience.title: Senior Merchandiser — Womenswear
bullets: Managed digital merchandising for womenswear category across UK and EU markets (GBP 45M annual GMV, 200+ brand partners)
Curated seasonal product assortments and homepage merchandising for Farfetch.com, balancing editorial vision with sell-through targets
Analysed conversion funnels, product page performance, and search ranking data using Farfetch internal analytics and Google Analytics 4
Collaborated with buying team on seasonal open-to-buy planning and markdown strategy for luxury ready-to-wear
Led personalised product recommendation project with data science team — increased average order value by 12%

experience.title: Merchandising Coordinator (promoted from Assistant, 2021)
bullets: Supported senior merchandisers with product uploads, categorisation, and taxonomy management across Farfetch marketplace
Managed product data quality for 15,000+ SKUs per season, ensuring accurate sizing, colour, and brand attribution
Created weekly sell-through reports and category performance dashboards using Looker
Coordinated with boutique partners on inventory availability and fulfilment SLAs

education: BA Fashion Management — London College of Fashion, UAL (2016-2019)

jd_requirements: luxury products codes storytelling emotional connection
5+ years digital merchandising e-commerce buying online retail
assortment planning fashion categories multiple markets
Salesforce Commerce Cloud SAP Hybris e-commerce platforms
deliberate_flaws: No Salesforce Commerce Cloud or SAP Hybris experience — used Farfetch's proprietary platform
No direct luxury brand experience (LVMH, Kering, Richemont) — only marketplace/multi-brand
Marketplace merchandising is different from DTC brand merchandising — different KPIs, different creative control
Laid off during Farfetch/Coupang restructuring — needs positive framing in interviews
No brand storytelling ownership — Farfetch merchandises other brands, doesn't create the brand narrative
No experience with single-brand e-commerce (Gucci.com is one brand, Farfetch is 200+)
No pricing strategy experience for luxury (Farfetch doesn't set prices, brands do)
No experience working with creative/design teams on product launches (merchandised existing collections)
No EMEA market experience beyond UK and EU (JD says EMEA which includes Middle East and Africa)

provenance.notes: Synthetic persona and JD. JD style modeled from real Greenhouse/Lever postings. Expected output and cloud_skills are AI-generated. 4/8 scorecard checks (jd_coverage, no_fabrication, metrics_preserved, no_fabricated_skills) evaluate AI vs AI reference. 4/8 checks (factual_preservation, word_count, ats_structure, action_verbs) are structurally valid regardless.
